title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security · Graph Lens: Untrusted Repository
summary: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git untrusted repository increment in a bounded Git automation and trust policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat hooks, filters, diff drivers, submodules, config includes, and build scripts as executable trust boundaries.
Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Untrusted Repository
steps.instruction:
theory case — A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Untrusted Repository contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for treat hooks, filters, diff drivers, submodules, config includes, and build scripts as executable trust boundaries.

content.text: Treat hooks, filters, diff drivers, submodules, config includes, and build scripts as executable trust boundaries. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Untrusted Repository while producing a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust. content.code:
# Worked focus: git-untrusted-repository — Treat hooks, filters, diff drivers, submodules, config includes, and build scripts as executable trust boundaries.
# Evidence: changed Git case 3
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
